task_updates.executor_notes: §5: Branch A verification names primary signal as zero new live_update_editor_runs rows under the legacy run-source after the flip timestamp + new run-source row on next tick, with the required one-line note 'requires Phase-1-provided run-source discriminator (TODO(phase-1) f); if absent at flip time, treat as third-mechanism case and ABORT per §1.' Branch B verification names Discord-side count as PRIMARY (operator scrolls live channel for window flip-to-now, confirms zero legacy-bot-identity posts per TODO(phase-1) d); SQL marker on legacy live_update_editor_runs row demoted to SECONDARY nice-to-have (TODO(phase-1) c), with explicit fallback 'If no marker shipped, the Discord-side count is the only signal — that is acceptable.' Cites src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py:84-107 _publish_accepted_candidates and :261 _select_publishable_candidates as the legacy fallback path. Explicit DO-NOT statement: 'Do not use "zero new rows in legacy live_update_feed_items" as a verification signal. Per :651, legacy table writes continue during the watch; only Discord-send is suppressed.' Procedure body has 8 numbered steps: (1) re-run §3; (2) set <LIVE_EDITOR_PUBLISH_TOGGLE> to TODO(phase-1) a value; (3) record flip timestamp as watch start and lower bound for subsequent SQL; (4) ANCHOR VERIFICATION ON NEXT OBSERVED RUN with polling SELECT against live_update_editor_runs where created_at > <flip timestamp>, waiting until status='completed' appears (~90 min max per hourly :633 cadence, jump to §8 otherwise); (5) verification SQL one-liner against topics scoped environment='prod' with last_published_at > flip timestamp grouped by publication_status; (6) confirm ≥1 'sent', zero 'partial', zero unexpected 'failed', jump to §6+§7 partial row on any partial; (7) confirm branch-appropriate signal — Branch A repeats step-4 query projecting the run-source discriminator (zero rows under legacy after flip), Branch B Discord-side scroll with SQL marker secondary; (8) any failure → §8, otherwise enter §7 watch. §6: four query blocks scoped environment='prod' with `-- swap to 'dev' for pre-flip verification (note: dev has no partials during Phase 1 per :611/:627/:633 — 0 dev partials does NOT mean prod is clean)` annotations — (a) UNION-ALL windowed counts for last_hour/last_24h/since_cutover on publication_status='partial'; (b) per-row deep-dive selecting topic_id, canonical_key, headline, state, publication_status, publication_error, publication_attempts, last_published_at, discord_message_ids ordered by last_published_at desc limit 50; (c) still-pending too long with NULL or older-than-90-minutes last_published_at; (d) rejection-rate against topic_transitions WHERE action LIKE 'rejected_%' last 1h grouped by action. Two-source trip-wire tally: prod-only SQL count `select count(*) as prod_partials_since_cutover from topics where environment='prod' and publication_status='partial' and last_published_at >= '<cutover-flip-timestamp>';` then explicit instruction 'Add the Phase-1 shadow-attempted-publish partial count from shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed in the Phase-1 monitoring channel + shadow logs per :611-644) — NOT from topics.' with the reasoning 'topics WHERE environment=dev AND publication_status=partial is vacuous; the shadow-attempted-publish counter is the only Phase-1 source.' Three operator-filled blanks (Phase-1 shadow partials, Phase-2 prod partials, sum). Trip-wire phrasing: 'fires when sum ≥ 2 per :600-601 → ABORT (§8) + restore topic_publications per :601.' References §7 partial-publish row for the same threshold. Schema-shape pointer: 'Column shapes per docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:117-123 and :223.'

task_updates.executor_notes: §7: Symptom|Threshold|Action|Pointer markdown table covering 15 rows. Partial-publish split into two rows: (1) single partial in cutover window → INVESTIGATE via §6(b); (2) '(Phase-1 telemetry shadow partials) + (Phase-2 prod partials from §6) cumulative ≥ 2 per :600-601 → ABORT + restore topic_publications + §8.' Rejection rate >50%/hr from §6(d) → TUNE then ABORT consult redesign citing :676. Collision-override rate >10% → TUNE per :603/:670/:673. Duplicate posts split into two rows (1 isolated → HOT-FIX check _publish_accepted_candidates idempotency at :84-107 + topic_sources uniqueness per :625; ≥2 recurring → ABORT). Legacy editor accidentally publishes → HOT-FIX re-verify toggle + branch-A/B signal from §5. Discord 429s/auth errors → HOT-FIX routing. LEASE SYMPTOMS SPLIT INTO THREE DISTINCT ROWS per :684: (i) new-editor self-contention on (environment, guild_id, live_channel_id) → HOT-FIX release lease + verify next tick; (ii) legacy editor writing to NEW tables (topics, topic_*) → ABORT (isolation broke per :651/:684); (iii) new + legacy run rows overlapping on same lease key → ABORT (lease misconfigured per :684). Shape divergence vs Phase 1 baseline split: explainable → ACCEPT pointing to §4; unexplained → TUNE/ABORT. record_observation <1/run sustained → INFO post-cutover concern per :602. Third-mechanism row: 'Phase 1 turns out to be a third mechanism (not Branch A or B) OR run-source discriminator (TODO(phase-1) f) absent → ABORT immediately — this runbook does not cover the cutover.' Footer: 'If a symptom is not listed, ABORT to rollback and consult docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md.' §8: two variants. Pre-rename rollback (5 steps): (1) flip <LIVE_EDITOR_PUBLISH_TOGGLE> back to legacy value; (2) drain new-editor in-flight run OR force-release lease for (environment, guild_id, live_channel_id) per :684; (3) wait for next scheduler tick (hourly per :633), confirm legacy publishes via _publish_accepted_candidates (live_update_editor.py:84-107 called from :261-346) — Branch A: new legacy run row appears under legacy run-source discriminator (TODO(phase-1) f); Branch B: post-rollback legacy run rows show suppression marker CLEARED (if Phase 1 writes one — TODO(phase-1) c) AND new live-channel post attributed to legacy bot identity (TODO(phase-1) d) as primary Discord-side signal; (4) verify new live_update_feed_items row AND corresponding Discord post; (5) 'Leave topics rows alone — do not truncate, do not rewrite' citing :596 with rationale that the runner-pointer flip is the rollback and new-editor topics state is durable for post-mortem. Post-rename rollback (rare): same toggle flip + reverse-rename migration mirror of §9 (six tables _legacy_<name> back to original names) authored and handed off via the same .migrations_staging/ → supabase/migrations/ → supabase db push → verify in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations chain documented in §9; then re-run pre-rename verification. Explicit statement at top of §8: 'Run §9 rename only after the 24-48h watch passes. This keeps rollback to a single toggle flip during the watch window.' Escalation: if rollback fails to restore legacy publishing within two scheduler ticks, page POM and consult the redesign doc.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. §7 splits lease symptoms into THREE DISTINCT ROWS: (i) new-editor self-contention HOT-FIX release lease for (environment, guild_id, live_channel_id) + verify next tick; (ii) legacy editor writing to NEW tables (topics, topic_*) → ABORT (isolation broke per :651/:684); (iii) new + legacy run rows overlapping on same lease key → ABORT (lease misconfigured per :684). Partial-publish trip-wire phrased exactly as '(Phase-1 telemetry shadow partials) + (Phase-2 prod partials from §6) cumulative ≥ 2 per :600-601 → ABORT + restore topic_publications' with pointer to §6 trip-wire and §8. Third-mechanism row explicitly covers 'Phase 1 turns out to be a third mechanism (not Branch A or B) OR run-source discriminator (TODO(phase-1) f) absent → ABORT immediately.' §8 leaves topics rows alone (step 5: 'Leave topics rows alone — do not truncate, do not rewrite' citing :596). Pre-rename rollback (single toggle flip + drain + next-tick verify) is distinct from post-rename rollback (toggle flip + reverse-rename migration via the §9 hand-off chain + re-run verification). Branch-A verification (new legacy run row under legacy run-source discriminator on next tick) and Branch-B verification (suppression marker CLEARED + new Discord post by legacy bot identity as primary signal) are both covered explicitly. :596 cited at the leave-topics-alone instruction.

Pre-flight constraint-preservation note: 'Postgres preserves indexes, sequences, FK constraints, and RLS across ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO — do not recreate them. Dependent views are NOT auto-updated.' Pre-check view query `select schemaname, viewname, definition from pg_views where definition ilike '%live_update_%';` with instruction to drop/recreate any matching views. Row-count parity SELECT before AND after as 6-table UNION ALL (counts must match exactly). Migration body: begin;...commit; transaction with ordered ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO for six legacy tables per docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223 (cross-checked against src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968 — NOT docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md which is about daily_summaries): live_update_feed_items, live_update_watchlist, live_update_duplicate_state, live_update_editorial_memory, live_update_candidates, live_update_decisions → _legacy_<name>. Post-flight row-count UNION ALL against _legacy_* names. Bring-forward circumstances (storage pressure, schema-drift, audit done early). Push-out circumstances (active forensics, regulator request, ongoing partial-publish investigation). DROP migration block: 'Use the same hand-off chain as §9' authored as 2026MMDDHHMMSS_live_update_legacy_drop.sql → supabase/migrations/ → supabase db push → verify in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations. begin;...commit; with DROP TABLE _legacy_<name>; for all six tables and rollback comment for dry-run. Explicit 'live_update_editor_runs is not dropped — same reason as §9.' Close-out checklist: legacy dropped + DROP migration recorded; runbook archived (move to docs/archive/ or note Phase 2 complete at top); docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md updated to note Phase 2 done (date, partial-publish-incident count, override-rate observed); trip-wire counters from :599-604 re-checked against first week of post-cutover data.
